audit.scope: Full repo: WPF/.NET 8 app, FEASolver.Core, Python scripts (Gmsh/CalculiX pipeline), tests, docs

environment.notes: CalculiX binary not installed (tools/ccx has README only) so end-to-end solve + verify_solver.py full path were not executed.

repo_health.linters_typecheckers: NONE configured (no ruff/flake8/mypy/black; no C# analyzers/.editorconfig)
repo_health.subprocess_security: OK (no shell=True in Python, UseShellExecute=false in C#, explicit exe paths)
repo_health.secrets_scan: No hardcoded secrets found in tracked files

issues.title: Principal-stress eigenvalue solver returns wrong values for general stress states
issues.description: The closed-form characteristic-polynomial solution is incorrectly constructed (uses a = -2*sqrt(r2) with cos-argument/assignment that do not match the depressed-cubic roots). Max/Min Principal stress is wrong for any tensor with shear components or repeated eigenvalues. A diagonal tensor with three distinct eigenvalues happens to return correct values, masking the bug.
issues.evidence: Ported the exact C# logic to Python and compared to numpy.linalg.eigvalsh: tensor(100,0,0,0,0,0) numpy[min0,max100] vs code[min-33.333,max66.667]; tensor(100,50,25,12;20,10,5) numpy[21.437,107.803] vs code[8.864,95.230].
issues.impact: Users viewing Max/Min Principal results get incorrect stress magnitudes (off by a constant offset proportional to the hydrostatic/shear content). Silent — no error thrown.
issues.repro: python: implement ComputePrincipal verbatim, compare against numpy.linalg.eigvalsh for any tensor with nonzero shear.
issues.suggested_fix: Replace with the canonical normalized-deviatoric trig algorithm: q=tr/3; p=sqrt((Σ(diag-q)^2 + 2Σshear^2)/6); B=(A-qI)/p; r=clamp(det(B)/2,-1,1); phi=acos(r)/3; max=q+2p*cos(phi); min=q+2p*cos(phi+2π/3). Verified to match numpy on all test tensors.
issues.tests_to_add: C# unit test ComputePrincipalTests comparing against precomputed eigenvalues for diagonal, sheared, hydrostatic, and degenerate tensors.

issues.title: C# test assembly does not compile — entire xUnit suite never runs
issues.description: ExportServiceTests.cs is missing 'using Xunit;' (the other three test files have it). Because [Fact] cannot resolve, the whole FEASolver.Tests project fails to build, so ExportServiceTests, ModelSerializationTests, MeshDataDeserializationTests, and ToolValidatorTests all fail to run.
issues.evidence: dotnet test FEASolver.sln -> CS0246 'FactAttribute could not be found' (x12) in ExportServiceTests.cs. Core builds cleanly; restore is up-to-date.
issues.impact: All C# regression coverage (export units/CSV, model serialization, mesh deserialization, tool validation) is currently dead. The '148/164 tests passing' claim covers only the Python suite.
issues.suggested_fix: Add 'using Xunit;' to ExportServiceTests.cs (or add a GlobalUsings.cs with 'global using Xunit;' for the test project).
issues.tests_to_add: Add 'dotnet build' of the test project to CI to prevent regressions of this class.

issues.title: Equivalent (von Mises) strain formula has wrong coefficient on normal-strain term
issues.description: Computes (2/3)*(0.5*Σdiff^2 + 2*Σshear^2) = (1/3)Σdiff^2 + (4/3)Σshear^2. The standard equivalent strain sqrt(2/3 ε_dev:ε_dev) requires (2/9)Σdiff^2 + (4/3)Σshear^2. The normal-difference term is overweighted by 50%.
issues.evidence: _strain_vonmises(e,-e/2,-e/2,0,0,0) returns 1.2247*e but the function's own docstring claims '=|ε_axial|' for incompressible uniaxial; correct value is e.
issues.impact: Strain von Mises field (optional/secondary output) is overstated wherever normal-strain differences dominate. Displacement and stress-von-Mises (primary outputs) are unaffected.
issues.suggested_fix: Use sqrt((2.0/9.0)*dev + (4.0/3.0)*shear) (equivalently sqrt(2/3 * ((1/3)dev + 2*shear))). Update docstring to match.
issues.tests_to_add: pytest asserting incompressible uniaxial -> |ε_axial|, and agreement with a numpy deviatoric reference.

issues.title: Potential pipe-buffer deadlock in INP-writer subprocess (stdout never drained)
issues.description: ProcessStartInfo sets RedirectStandardOutput=true but only StandardError is read (ReadToEndAsync). If write_inp.py ever emits enough to fill the stdout pipe buffer, the child blocks on write while the parent blocks on WaitForExit -> deadlock. MeshingService.RunPythonAsync drains both streams concurrently and is the correct pattern.
issues.impact: Latent hang; low probability today because write_inp.py logs to stderr and prints little to stdout, but brittle to future changes.
issues.repro: Add a large stdout dump to write_inp.py and run a solve.
issues.suggested_fix: Drain stdout and stderr concurrently (Task.WhenAll on both ReadToEndAsync) before WaitForExitAsync, mirroring MeshingService.

issues.title: No CI pipeline; tests are not gated on commits/PRs
issues.description: Repository has no CI configuration. The prompt requires CI to run lint, tests, static analysis, and a regression job. The C# build break (AUDIT-002) would have been caught immediately by CI.
issues.impact: Regressions (incl. non-compiling tests) ship undetected; no enforced quality gate.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a GitHub Actions workflow: matrix job running 'dotnet build' + 'dotnet test' and 'pip install -r requirements.txt' + 'pytest', plus ruff and dotnet format --verify-no-changes. Add a dedicated regression-test job.

issues.title: Dependency drift and weak pinning break reproducibility
issues.description: requirements.txt pins numpy==1.26.4 and pytest==8.2.0 and gmsh>=4.12.2 (open upper bound). The audit environment runs numpy 2.2.6, gmsh 4.15.2, pytest 9.0.3, Python 3.13 — while claude.mb documents Python 3.11 / numpy 1.26.4. Tests pass on the newer stack, but the documented and actual environments diverge and there is no lockfile.
issues.impact: Non-reproducible builds; a future numpy/gmsh major bump could silently change meshing or numeric results.
issues.suggested_fix: Pin tested versions (or add a lockfile / constraints file), reconcile requirements.txt with claude.mb, and add an OSV/safety scan to CI.

issues.title: Poisson-ratio validation excludes valid ν=0 and auxetic materials
issues.description: Requires 0 < ν < 0.5, rejecting ν=0 (valid, e.g. cork-like / test cases) and negative ν (auxetic, valid in (-1,0.5)). Thermodynamic admissible range for isotropic linear elasticity is -1 < ν < 0.5.
issues.suggested_fix: Allow -1 < ν < 0.5 (CalculiX requires ν<0.5 strictly; ν=0 acceptable).

issues.title: FRD float regex assumes 3-digit exponent (Windows-specific)
issues.description: Primary regex requires exactly 5 mantissa digits and 3 exponent digits ([+-][0-9]{3}). CalculiX builds on non-Windows platforms typically emit 2-digit exponents, so the primary match fails there and the code relies on the sign-boundary fallback. Acceptable for a Windows app but undocumented as a portability constraint and untested for the 2-digit path.
issues.impact: Reduced robustness on non-Windows ccx output; fallback mitigates but is less precise for adjacent negatives.
issues.suggested_fix: Relax to E[+-][0-9]{2,3} and add a regression test with a 2-digit-exponent FRD fixture.

issues.title: No linting or type-checking configured for either language
issues.description: No ruff/flake8/black/mypy config for Python and no .editorconfig/analyzers/nullable-warnings-as-errors policy for C#. Style and type drift are unenforced.
issues.suggested_fix: Add ruff + mypy (Python) and .editorconfig + TreatWarningsAsErrors / analyzers (C#); wire into CI.

issues.title: Doc drift: stated test count and architecture pointer out of date
issues.description: ship_readiness.md and claude.mb state '148 Python tests'; actual is 164. docs/architecture.md is a bare pointer to claude.mb. Minor but erodes trust in the docs.
issues.suggested_fix: Update counts; consider generating the test count in CI.

issues.title: Unquoted ccx job-name argument
issues.description: Arguments = $"-i {inputName}" is not quoted. inputName is hardcoded to 'model' today so it is safe, but the pattern is brittle if a caller ever passes a name with spaces.
issues.suggested_fix: Quote the job name or assert it matches ^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.

verified_correct: Torque->nodal-force distribution (r^2-weighted) — Σ(r×F)·a = T validated analytically in code and tests.
C3D10 consistent (Galerkin) surface lumping: corners=0, midsides=A/3 — analytically correct for 6-node triangle.
Gmsh TET10 -> CCX C3D10 midside swap (slots 8<->9) consistent with inp_writer _FACE_MIDSIDES mapping.
Explicit unit handling (mm/m -> SI metres) with no fragile bbox heuristic.
Subprocess hygiene: no shell, explicit exe paths, STEP path-traversal/extension guard.
FRD fixed-width float parser handles negative-sign column shift via format-matching regex with fallbacks.
